title: 2-3 additional flagship program guides for the pilot jurisdiction (cited, expert-reviewed)
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. This task adds 2-3 more program guides for the pilot jurisdiction at the same bar as the flagship: primary-source-cited, reading-level compliant, route-to-human coverage, expert-signed-off, with no determination/structuring content and immigration-attorney sign-off where non-citizen eligibility is addressed. The specific additional programs are TO BE SECURED (selected after the flagship, gated on reviewer availability).
objective: Write 2-3 additional flagship-quality program guides for the pilot jurisdiction, each cited, reading-level compliant, route-to-human covered, and expert-signed-off.
acceptanceCriteria: Each new program guide meets the same bar as the flagship: primary-source-cited, reading-level compliant, route-to-human coverage, expert-signed-off, no determination/structuring content
Immigration-attorney sign-off recorded where non-citizen eligibility is addressed
Each guide carries the fixed not-advice + non-partisan framing and emits an official application link plus free human help pointers
